rationale: No-regression lens, focused on the new internal/validate test-import in ownership_test.go. Confirmed no import cycle: `go list -test -deps ./internal/lifecycle` shows internal/validate only as a test-only dependency edge, and `go list -deps ./internal/validate` (and grep of its non-test imports) shows no reference to internal/lifecycle anywhere in its transitive closure. Production internal/lifecycle package itself (non-test) does not import internal/validate. `go build ./...` succeeds. `go test -count=1 ./internal/lifecycle ./internal/setup ./cmd/mindspec` all green, including TestLifecycleOwnershipExactlyOneWorkflowClaimant and the new TestClaimsLifecyclePackageDiscrimination (all 4 subtests pass, verified via -v). gofmt -l is clean on both changed Go files. The claude.go diff is confirmed doc-only: it only rewords/appends string-literal content inside the embedded skill markdown (no control flow, no signature, no logic touched). The ADR/skill diffs and ownership_test.go changes are additive (new const, new helper, new subtests) plus one matcher swap (strings.HasPrefix -> validate.GlobMatch); no existing test's assertion or expected value was altered to mask a change. AC9 substring counts (`impl approve` in ADR, `closed without` in all 3 skill surfaces) all >=1. No regression found.
findings: internal/validate does not import internal/lifecycle (verified via go list -deps and direct grep of non-test imports) -- no cycle.
internal/lifecycle (production, non-test) does not import internal/validate; the new import is confined to ownership_test.go (test-only edge), confirmed via `go list -test -deps`.
go build ./... succeeds; go vet clean on internal/lifecycle, internal/validate, internal/setup, cmd/mindspec.

TestClaimsLifecyclePackageDiscrimination's 4 subtests (broader internal/** glob, exact duplicate, workflow-removed, narrower sibling) all pass, demonstrating the GlobMatch-based matcher genuinely discriminates.
TestLifecycleOwnershipExactlyOneWorkflowClaimant still passes against real repo manifests.
gofmt -l reports no issues on ownership_test.go or claude.go.
claude.go change is confined to a Go string literal (embedded skill doc text) -- no logic/signature change.
No existing test assertion/expected value was edited; only additive subtests plus the matcher body swap in ownership_test.go.
